briefing.incidentSummary:
A family noticed their internet was unusually slow for weeks.

A network scan revealed an unknown device had been connected to their home WiFi — downloading large files late at night.

The device was traced to someone nearby.
briefing.missionText:
Your Mission:

› Identify the unauthorized device

› Trace it to its owner

› Collect at least 3 correct evidences

› Flag the right suspect

suspects.name: Neil V
suspects.profileNotes: Device MAC address registered to a laptop purchased under Neil V's name. Downloads averaged 4GB per night — mostly pirated movies.

suspects.name: Rohan D
suspects.profileNotes: Connected briefly while making a delivery. Only 2MB data used. No suspicious activity.

suspects.name: Preethi A
suspects.profileNotes: Has the WiFi password legitimately. Only visits on weekends. No nighttime activity.

items.label: router_access_log.txt
items.detail: Router log shows device MAC 44:D9:E7:2A:BC:01 connected every night between 12:00 AM and 3:00 AM for 18 consecutive days. Total data consumed: 72GB.

items.label: mac_address_lookup.txt
items.detail: MAC address 44:D9:E7:2A:BC:01 is registered to a Dell Inspiron laptop sold to Neil V, Flat 4B, on 12 March 2024 per retailer records.

items.label: bandwidth_report.pdf
items.detail: ISP bandwidth report shows 4GB average nightly usage between 12–3 AM. Daytime usage by family accounts for only 0.8GB/day — clearly two separate users.

hiddenItem.label: dhcp_hostname_log.txt
hiddenItem.detail: DHCP server assigned hostname 'NEIL-LAPTOP' to MAC 44:D9:E7:2A:BC:01. Device name directly identifies the owner.

minigame.title: Decode Device Hostname
minigame.hint: Caesar shift -3. What is the device's hostname?
hints: It is the name of a person followed by a device type.

onSuccess.message: dhcp_hostname_log.txt is now available in your file feed.

items.label: Signal Strength Log
items.detail: The unauthorized device consistently showed -48 dBm signal strength — indicating it was within 10 meters of the router. Flat 4B is directly adjacent.

items.label: Connection Timing
items.detail: Device connected exactly at midnight every night — suggesting an automated scheduled task, not casual browsing.

items.label: Unknown Device IP
items.detail: IP 192.168.0.109 was dynamically assigned to the unauthorized device. All large download traffic routes through this IP.

timeline.title: Family Notices Slowdown
timeline.description: Internet speed drops significantly during evening

timeline.title: Network Scan Run
timeline.description: Family runs scan, finds unknown device

perfect.title: Case Solved!
perfect.subtitle: Neil V identified as the WiFi thief.
perfect.label: CASE CLOSED

wrongAccusation.title: Wrong Suspect
wrongAccusation.subtitle: The real culprit was Neil V.
wrongAccusation.label: CASE FAILED

aria.welcomeToHub:
...Someone was stealing bandwidth. Every night. For 18 days.
They thought no one was watching.
Prove them wrong.
aria.exploreFeeds:
Router logs don't lie.
MAC addresses, signal strength, hostnames.
The device left a full trail.
aria.viewEvidence:
Every device has a fingerprint.
This one leads somewhere very close.
aria.markEvidence: Good find. Keep building the chain.
aria.decryptionHint:
The hostname is encoded.
Decode it — it might have a name in it.
aria.flagSuspect:
A trace is forming.
You're on to something — keep pulling the thread.
More fragments remain. Don't stop now.
